source.title: Fortress Defensive Setup Guide (Commanders/Bathroom, Dorms/Games, Cafe/Waiting)
source.notes: Creator is confident on the top-floor sites (Commanders/Bathroom and Dorms/Games) but still learning the ground-floor Cafe/Waiting site, calling that section a work in progress — treat Cafe/Waiting entries as lower-confidence than the top two. The fourth site, Ham, is explicitly skipped/not covered and has no entry in this file. Auto-generated captions with several ASR ambiguities on operator/player names — flagged inline below rather than guessed. Timestamps are the nearest transcript chapter mark (captions are only chunked in ~20s blocks, not per-sentence). Location strings are room/feature names only, no compass prefixes: no labeled top-down blueprint with room-name callouts could be sourced for Fortress (Ubisoft's official blueprint zip and other map sites either have no text labels or aren't text-accessible), so compass directions were not invented per rule 8.

reinforcements.location: Commanders Wall Toward Horse, Left Side
reinforcements.note: Hold both sides from here. Don't reinforce this and the neighbouring wall both.

reinforcements.location: Back Commanders Wall (Renovation Stairs Side), Left Side
reinforcements.note: Leave this side as a rotate at round start; reinforce later once you've absorbed drones/utility.

reinforcements.location: Wall by the Eagle/Main Stairs corridor (leads to Bedroom breach and Commanders breach)
reinforcements.note: Soft wall — Bandit battery here can be shot from below, not a fully safe trick spot. Hold this corridor/rotate early to force drones and utility, then fall back and reinforce.
Reinforce alongside the left side; put the rotate on the bottom right of the same wall instead.
Second reinforcement on the same wall.

headHoles.location: Commanders Wall Toward Horse, Right Side
headHoles.note: Paired with the left-side reinforcement — defend into Horse without giving up full room control.
Paired with a rotate on the right side of the same wall.
Spot/kill drones dropping from the roof here or through the vending machine window; covers the exact landing spot.
Left soft; paired with the reinforced right side of the same wall.

rotates.location: Back Commanders Wall (Renovation Stairs Side), Right Side
rotates.note: Rotate only at round start — reinforcing both this and the neighbouring wall traps a defender with no way out. Absorb drones/utility for 20-30 seconds, concede, fall back and reinforce later.

rotates.location: Soft wall between Commanders and Bathroom
rotates.note: Direct rotate between the two rooms that make up the site.
Same early-rotate-then-late-reinforce approach as the Commanders-side rotate.
Paired with reinforcing the left and right sides of the same wall.
Paired with head holes on the left side of the same wall.

hatches.note: Leave open, don't reinforce. Creates a crossing problem for attackers.

gadgetPlacements.location: One of the corridor lights near Horse/Museum
gadgetPlacements.note: Prox-alarm spot — sits on the far side of the light, out of attackers' view, for early warning before the side door.

gadgetPlacements.location: Horse/Museum area, both sides of the door
gadgetPlacements.operator: Unconfirmed — source audio names an operator phonetically as 'wai' here, which doesn't clearly match a real defender (possibly intended as Wamai, but treat as unconfirmed rather than guessed)
gadgetPlacements.note: Shield operator here plus this second operator covering both sides of the door, with prox alarms on the door/stairs/corridor approaches.
Anchor point. Put barbed wire or a prox alarm here for notification of anyone pushing up the stairs, then defend the corridor and both breach walls from this position.

gadgetPlacements.location: Briefing/Dorms door area (the main and only door into this pocket)
gadgetPlacements.note: Hold this single door to shut down drone paths through it; reuse any drone taken here.

Bandit trick, Kaid trick, or a third possible trick source audio names phonetically as 'tuberc tricked' (doesn't match a real operator/trick name — treat as unconfirmed) on the two door-wall reinforcements here. Watch the nearby window — attackers grenade-bounce off the wall from there to knock defenders off a Bandit trick.

operatorRecommendations.note: Paired with a shield operator holding Horse/Museum; possibly Wamai given the door-holding context, but not confirmed — verify against the video directly before relying on this.
Protects the soft-wall batteries near the renovation stairs corridor and the Bathroom triple wall door reinforcements. The renovation stairs battery can be shot from below.
Alternative to Bandit for tricking the Bathroom triple wall reinforcements.

operatorRecommendations.holdLocation: Old Tower / Games, falling back to Briefing
operatorRecommendations.note: Creator's own solo-queue persona ('Mozy'/'Mozzy' — the creator's callsign, not an R6 operator) demonstrates: absorb drones from Old Tower/Games, retreat layer by layer toward Briefing, push back with intel advantage.

generalTips.note: Learn this site first — top-priority site on the map.

Defend in layers: don't sit in site from round start. Start further out (renovation/eagle stairs corridor), absorb drones and force attackers to spend utility (frags, Ying candelas, Grim canisters), then concede space and fall back to reinforce behind you.
Bedroom (not a bombsite itself) connects both site doors — defend the wall into Commanders, the head holes toward Horse, and the wall into Bathroom from here.

Watch for: attackers open the left-hand wall into Bathroom, then plant behind the half-wall there using three attackers — one covering the corridor swing angle, one holding near the open breach, one on smoke/sense covering the plant. Alternative plant spot is tight against the door, cutting off the Horse-door angle.
Requires more active layered defense than most other sites on the map.

reinforcements.location: Single wall looking toward Old Tower
Left side of the Games room wall, out toward the vending area/window

reinforcements.note: Paired with a rotate on the left plus head holes.

reinforcements.location: Both door walls (near the games/walk-in doors)
reinforcements.note: Bandit-trick, Kaid-trick, or the source audio's unconfirmed third 'trick' name. Watch the nearby window — attackers can grenade-bounce off the wall from there to knock a trick operator off.

reinforcements.location: Wall next to the Dorms breach (undecided)
reinforcements.note: Currently leaning toward NOT reinforcing this — leave only the locker-swing angle to cover.

headHoles.location: Old Tower stairs drop zone (Dorms/Games side)
headHoles.note: Spot/punish drones dropping from the roof; angle on the exact landing spot.
Peek into Games from this door while covering Dorms.
Paired with the reinforced right side and a rotate on the same wall.

rotates.location: Late reinforcement rotate near the dorms/games fallback layer
rotates.note: Keep as a rotate early, reinforce late only if needed while falling back.
Small hole, not a full rotate-then-reinforce — catch early drones then fall back. Lets whoever's Bandit-tricking the door walls also cover the Vending Machine angle and peek the single wall.

gadgetPlacements.location: Vending machine area (in front of Old Tower drop)
gadgetPlacements.operator: Melusi or Kapkan-style trap pick (unconfirmed — source audio says 'a couple of keepers or a keepa barrier... and then a ka barrier', which doesn't clearly match a real operator/gadget name; treat as unconfirmed rather than guessed)
gadgetPlacements.note: Most important area to defend on this site. Barrier-style gadget covering the Old Tower drop angle plus a second covering the vending machine window jump-in, or a deployable shield played from slightly behind it if no barrier operator is available.

gadgetPlacements.location: Doorway near the vending machine shield spot
gadgetPlacements.note: ADS (Jäger) here early round to shoot down grenades thrown at a deployable shield covering this angle.

gadgetPlacements.location: Behind flight cases near the bandit-trick window
gadgetPlacements.note: Safe crouch spot while Bandit-tricking — stay crouched to avoid being domed through the window; you can still peek out from here.

operatorRecommendations.note: Tricks the two door-wall reinforcements near Games/Walk-in; use the flight-cases crouch spot near the window for safety.
Alternative to Bandit for tricking the same door walls.
Covers the Old Tower drop and vending machine window jump-in with barrier gadgets; verify the specific operator against the video before relying on this.

generalTips.note: Defend in layers: start out near Old Tower/vending, fall back through the Games door layer, then back into the site to reinforce.
Don't give up the Old Tower/vending machine area too early — conceding immediately gives attackers multiple breach angles with too much free space.

Briefing covers both site entrances (from Old Tower and from Central Stairs). Attackers occasionally push the long way around through Bedroom/Bathroom into Briefing instead of the more common Old Tower route.
Slightly harder site than Commanders/Bathroom due to the external breach into Dorms.

Watch for: attackers open the Dorms wall, push around via the window/stairs from Horse with a smoke or sense projector, plant tucked in near the flight cases. Alternative, more exposed plant spot is further into the room toward Briefing. Pros have also taken Big Tower and planted in the far corner behind an ROU/sense curtain.

reinforcements.note: Reinforce both of the double Cafe walls facing Infirmary.

reinforcements.location: Single wall from Waiting into Infirmary
reinforcements.note: Reinforce this wall (of the three walls visible from inside Infirmary).

reinforcements.location: Single wall from Cafe out to the main hallway corridor

reinforcements.note: Reinforcing all three currently — unresolved/work-in-progress, may switch to head holes here later. Lowest-confidence call in this file.

headHoles.note: Leave soft with head holes — covers the Infirmary window and door.

rotates.location: Random soft wall in the middle of Cafe
rotates.note: Easy movement around the site; peek toward the room leading out to Laundry.

hatches.location: Hatch in Briefing (upstairs), dropping down into Infirmary
hatches.note: Hold and drop through/play off. Most of the ground-floor plant happens down in Waiting below it.
Second hatch on this side of the map.

gadgetPlacements.note: Angle the shield across rather than blocking the doorway directly, so you can still defend if the surrounding breaches get opened.

generalTips.note: Lower-confidence site than Commanders/Bathroom or Dorms/Games — setup here is a work in progress and may change.

Track verticality: the ceiling above Waiting is solid floor, but large sections above other parts of the site (visible from Briefing/Games above) are destructible floor attackers can drop or shoot through.
Infirmary is the most important room on this site — gives attackers cover to open both the Waiting wall and the Cafe wall from a hidden position.
Ham (fourth site on this floor) is not covered here and not recommended for play — hard to defend, easy to attack, reachable via a soft wall near the site.
